reasoning: A 50 bps interest rate cut in a single scheduled FOMC meeting is historically rare, usually occurring only during sharp economic slowdowns, crises, or emergency recalibrations (e.g., 2001, 2008, 2020, and Sep 2024). The market estimate of 0.09 on Polymarket heavily guides this forecast, implying roughly a 1-in-11 chance of such aggressive easing by January 2026. Without recent major shock indicators forcing a deviation, sticking to the market consensus is the most sensible baseline.
